stories.title: Murakami to IL — White Sox Lose AL HR Co-Leader to Hamstring (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami is on the injured list. ⚾

Grade 2 right hamstring strain. Expected timeline: four weeks.

He had 20 home runs — tied for the AL lead. The best rookie story in baseball just got put on hold.

#WhiteSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
With Murakami on the IL, Kyle Schwarber now holds sole possession of the MLB home run lead.

He hit his 20th homer on the earliest date in MLB history. He's on pace for 66.

The race just got a lot more interesting. ⚾

#Phillies #MLB #HomeRuns

facebook.long_form:
The best rookie story in baseball just got put on pause.

Munetaka Murakami — the Japanese import who had taken the American League by storm with 20 home runs — was placed on the injured list Saturday with a Grade 2 right hamstring strain. White Sox GM Chris Getz confirmed the severity and projected a four-week recovery timeline.

The injury happened Friday night while Murakami was legging out a grounder. He exited almost immediately, and by Saturday it was confirmed: he's out.

Murakami had 20 home runs — tied for the AL lead — and was the clear frontrunner for AL Rookie of the Year. He's been one of the most fun players in baseball to watch in 2026. The White Sox called up Jacob Gonzalez from Triple-A to fill the roster spot.

The good news for Chicago: they won Saturday anyway, beating the Tigers 7-1. But a White Sox team fighting for an AL Wild Card spot without their best player is a very different team than the one that's been rolling.

The 2026 home run race just lost one of its biggest participants. Four weeks is an eternity in a pennant race. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Munetaka Murakami placed on IL. Grade 2 hamstring strain. Out ~4 weeks. 20 HRs go on pause. ⚾ #WhiteSox #MLB

stories.title: Tanner Scott Blows First Save of 2026 — Phillies 4-3 Dodgers (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Tanner Scott had not allowed a run in 13 straight appearances.

Then Edmundo Sosa hit a 2-run homer in the 8th. Bryce Harper added an RBI single. Phillies 4, Dodgers 3. ⚾

First blown save of 2026 for Scott. Dodgers' 6-game win streak snapped.

#Phillies #Dodgers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
For 13 consecutive appearances, Tanner Scott was untouchable. 1.14 ERA. 0 runs allowed since April 24. The Dodgers' closer was one of the best relievers in baseball.

Then came the eighth inning on Saturday night.

Roki Sasaki had been tremendous — 5.1 innings, 7 strikeouts, just the one Alec Bohm homer in the second. The Dodgers led 3-1 entering the eighth and Scott jogged in to close it out.

He allowed a pair of singles. Then, with two outs, Edmundo Sosa crushed a 2-run homer just over the left field wall. Tied. Bryce Harper added an RBI single to finish it off. Phillies 4, Dodgers 3.

First blown save of 2026 for Scott. Dodgers' 6-game win streak, snapped. Series, tied at 1-1.

This was a great game by any measure — Sasaki looked like a legitimate ace, and the comeback required two-out clutch hitting at its finest. But the one thing everyone will remember is Scott's first blown save of the year, and the brief flashback it created to his 10-blown-save nightmare in 2025.

Game 3 is today. Dodger Stadium. The series gets decided. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Sosa's 2-run homer. Scott's first blown save of 2026. Phillies 4, Dodgers 3. Series tied heading to today's rubber game. ⚾ #Phillies #Dodgers #MLB

stories.title: MLB HR Race Update — Schwarber Leads MLB Alone After Murakami IL (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The 2026 MLB home run race entering June:

⚾ Schwarber (PHI): MLB lead — on pace for 66
⚾ Murakami (CWS): On IL (20 HRs, hamstring, ~4 wks)
⚾ Judge (NYY): Chasing from behind

Earliest 20-HR date in MLB history. Is this the year someone hits 60+ again?

#MLB #HomeRuns

facebook.long_form:
Let's check in on the most compelling storyline in baseball.

Kyle Schwarber already did something nobody had done in MLB history — he hit his 20th home run on the earliest date ever, besting Luis Gonzalez's mark from 2001. He's on pace for approximately 66 home runs. He leads Major League Baseball.

And now, with Munetaka Murakami sidelined four weeks, Schwarber holds that lead alone.

The 2026 home run race had been building toward something special: Murakami (20 HRs, best rookie since Shohei) vs. Schwarber (historic pace) vs. Aaron Judge (always lurking). Now one of those three is watching from the trainer's room for the next month.

That means Schwarber has an opening — and the numbers he's putting up suggest he doesn't need one.

Is 2026 the year someone finally hits 60+ home runs again? The last time it happened was Barry Bonds in 2001. The exclusive club — Bonds (73), McGwire (70), Sosa (66, 65, 63), McGwire again (70) — has been untouched for over two decades.

Kyle Schwarber is on pace. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Schwarber leads MLB in HRs. On pace for 66. The earliest 20-HR date in history. Is 60 coming? ⚾ #Phillies #MLB #HomeRuns

stories.title: Phillies-Dodgers Game 3 Today — Series Winner in a Rubber Matchup (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Phillies and Dodgers are tied at 1-1. Rubber game is today at Dodger Stadium. ⚾

Dodgers need a bounce-back after Tanner Scott's first blown save of the year.

Phillies have the momentum after a three-run 8th inning comeback.

Series winner takes it today. #Phillies #Dodgers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Phillies and Dodgers. Tied at 1-1. Rubber game today at Dodger Stadium.

Game 1 went to the Dodgers — a 4-2 win with their offense doing the work. Game 2 went to the Phillies after Tanner Scott's first blown save of 2026 and a three-run eighth inning.

Now it's a one-game decider.

The Dodgers need a bounce-back. Roki Sasaki threw 5.1 innings yesterday — he won't be back. The bullpen needs to be better. The offense needs to finish what they start.

The Phillies want to close out a marquee series win. Kyle Schwarber and Bryce Harper in an LA stadium with momentum — it's not a bad spot to be in.

Whatever happens today sets the tone for both teams heading into June. Neither team can afford to drop this series. Both need to make a statement.

Rubber game baseball. There's nothing better. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Game 3. Tied 1-1. Dodger Stadium. Today. Series winner takes it all. ⚾ #Phillies #Dodgers #MLB

stories.title: Sunday MLB Watch Guide — Cubs-Cardinals SNB + Standings Check (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Sunday MLB schedule — big day: ⚾

• Cubs vs. Cardinals — 7 PM ET Sunday Night Baseball
• Phillies-Dodgers Game 3 — series rubber match
• Mariners-D'backs — 4:10 PM Peacock

15 games total. Pick your poison.

#MLB #Baseball #SundayBaseball

facebook.long_form:
Happy Sunday. Here's your complete guide to today's MLB action. 15 games. Here's what to watch:

🍺 **Cubs vs. Cardinals — 7 PM ET, Sunday Night Baseball**
The NL Central rivals meet in a marquee Sunday Night Baseball broadcast. The Cubs have been one of the best stories in the NL this season. The Cardinals are still alive in the Central. This one matters.

⚾ **Phillies vs. Dodgers Game 3 — Dodger Stadium**
Already broken down above — series rubber game. Two NL powerhouses, momentum in play.

📺 **Mariners vs. Diamondbacks — 4:10 PM ET, Peacock**
The Mariners have been quietly building one of the better stories in the AL West. This series finale matters for their positioning.

🏟️ **Rays vs. Angels — afternoon game**
Tampa Bay trying to hold their AL East lead in a divisional race that's been tight all year.

One more note heading into June: Murakami is on the IL. Schwarber leads MLB in HRs. The standings are getting serious. The race is on. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Sunday MLB: Cubs vs. Cardinals at 7 PM for Sunday Night Baseball. Plus Phillies-Dodgers rubber game and 13 more. Your whole day, planned. ⚾ #MLB #SundayBaseball
